task_completion.description: Create a test order for customer 'janesmith'
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the Drupal Commerce admin interface (e.g., /admin/commerce/orders) with a newly created order assigned to the customer 'janesmith' (or jane.smith@example.com).

task_completion.description: Create a view block named 'My Recent Orders'
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the Views configuration page (/admin/structure/views) showing a new view or block display titled 'My Recent Orders'.

task_completion.description: Configure the block to include Order ID, Placed date, Total price, and State fields
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the Views UI 'Fields' section showing exactly these fields added: Order ID, Placed date, Total price, and State (or Order state).

task_completion.description: Configure the view to show the 5 most recent orders for the specific user being viewed
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the Views UI showing a Contextual Filter (e.g., User ID from URL or Customer UID), a Sort criterion (e.g., Placed date descending), and the Pager settings limited to a specified number of items (5).

task_completion.description: Place the block in the Content region and restrict visibility to user profile pages
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the Block Layout configuration (/admin/structure/block) showing 'My Recent Orders' placed in the 'Content' region, with the Pages visibility restriction set to '/user/*'.

task_completion.description: Verify the block's functionality by visiting Jane Smith's profile
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot of Jane Smith's user profile page (URL ending in /user/3) displaying the 'My Recent Orders' block in the main content area, containing the test order created earlier with the correct fields.

integrity.description: Agent used the Drupal GUI to create the order, view, and block
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent navigating the Drupal admin interface in a web browser, rather than using a terminal to run Drush commands or directly editing YAML configuration files.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual application
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show standard browser UI, page loading states, and authentic Drupal admin forms being filled out, not a text editor or mocked HTML pages.

integrity.description: Agent configured dynamic filtering rather than hardcoding the block for user 3
integrity.visual_evidence: The Views configuration should show a contextual filter (e.g., User ID from URL) rather than a standard filter hardcoded to 'janesmith' or UID 3.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The test user 'janesmith' has UID 3. The view machine name should be 'my_recent_orders'. The block visibility path pattern must be exactly '/user/*'. The final verification must be performed on the path '/user/3'.
